artifacts.title: Launch Video Brief
artifacts.content:
# Launch Video Brief

Goal: produce a 45-second launch video for the Northstar workspace release.

Creative intent:
- calm, editorial motion
- no handheld camera simulation
- still frames are allowed only for the final CTA card

Provider lock:
- after approval, provider must stay on Remotion + ImageGen Alpha
- sample-before-batch generation is mandatory before full asset production

artifacts.title: Launch Video Script
artifacts.content:
# Script

Scene 1 introduces the pain of tab overload.
Scene 2 shows the workspace timeline.
Scene 3 ends with the CTA line: 'Ship context, not chaos.'

The narration must keep the phrase 'sample-before-batch' as a production rule reminder.

artifacts.title: Scene Plan
artifacts.content:
# Scene Plan

Scene 1:
- duration: 10s
- motion required: yes
- visual: browser tabs collapsing into a single timeline rail

Scene 2:
- duration: 18s
- motion required: yes
- visual: cards snapping into a production board

Scene 3:
- duration: 17s
- motion required: no
- visual: still CTA card with product lockup

artifacts.title: Asset Manifest
artifacts.content:
# Asset Manifest

Required assets:
- 3 product UI stills
- 2 gradient backplates
- 1 voiceover track
- 1 logo lockup SVG

Batching rule:
- generate one approved keyframe sample before producing the full image batch
- keep provider fixed to ImageGen Alpha after approval

artifacts.title: Render Report
artifacts.content:
# Render Report

Terminal output: final Remotion render at 1920x1080, 24fps.

Checks:
- provider remained locked after approval
- still image used only on the final CTA card
- render completed without missing assets

evaluations.query: Which provider stack stays locked after approval for the launch video?
What is the final CTA line in the approved script?
Which scene is allowed to stay still instead of requiring motion?
What batching rule applies before generating the full image set?
What is the terminal output deliverable for the pipeline?
